app.title: 🎛️ ACE-Step V1.5 Playground💡
app.subtitle: Pushing the Boundaries of Open-Source Music Generation

dataset.title: 📊 Dataset Explorer
dataset.search_value_placeholder: Enter keys or index (leave empty for random)
dataset.search_value_info: Keys: exact match, Index: 0 to dataset size-1.
dataset.use_src_info: Check to use the source audio from dataset.

service.title: 🔧 Service Configuration
service.checkpoint_info: The model weights file to load. 'turbo' models are faster (4–8 steps), 'base' models are slower but support more features (guidance scale, extract/lego modes). Pick turbo for quick generation, base for maximum control.
service.model_path_info: The directory containing model config and architecture files. Auto-detected from your checkpoint selection. Only change this if you have a custom model setup.
service.device_info: Which hardware to run on. 'auto' picks the best available (recommended). 'cuda' = NVIDIA GPU, 'mps' = Apple Silicon, 'xpu' = Intel GPU, 'cpu' = very slow, last resort.
service.lm_model_path_info: The language model that powers Think mode, CoT metadata, caption enhancement, and semantic code generation. Required for Think mode. Larger LMs produce better results but need more VRAM.
service.backend_info: Engine for the 5Hz LM. vLLM = significantly faster inference, recommended for NVIDIA GPUs with ≥8GB VRAM. PyTorch (pt) = slower but works on all hardware (CPU, MPS, XPU). Use pt if vLLM won't install or you're on non-NVIDIA hardware.
service.init_llm_info: Load the 5Hz LM into memory on startup. Required for: Think mode, CoT metadata generation, caption/lyrics enhancement, and Auto Score. Uses additional VRAM (~2–6GB depending on model size). Disable if you only need basic generation without LM features.
service.lm_unavailable_vram: ⚠️ LM not available for this GPU tier (VRAM too low)
service.flash_attention_info_enabled: Speeds up DiT inference by ~20–30% with lower memory usage. No quality difference — purely a performance optimization. Recommended if available. Requires the flash_attn package.
service.flash_attention_info_disabled: Flash attention not available (flash_attn package not installed). Install with: pip install flash-attn. Provides ~20–30% speedup with no quality change.
service.offload_cpu_info: Moves models to system RAM when not actively running, freeing GPU VRAM for the active model. Adds a small delay (~1–3s) when switching between models. Essential for GPUs with <16GB VRAM. Negligible impact on GPUs with >24GB.
service.offload_dit_cpu_info: Also offloads the DiT model to CPU (in addition to the base offload). Saves more VRAM but adds extra swap time. Requires 'Offload to CPU' to be enabled. Use on very low VRAM GPUs (<10GB).
service.compile_model_info: Optimizes the model using PyTorch compilation for faster inference. First generation takes longer (compilation overhead), subsequent generations are ~10–20% faster. Required if you want to use INT8 quantization. Recommended to keep enabled.
service.quantization_info: Reduces model weights from 32-bit to 8-bit, cutting VRAM usage by ~50% with minimal quality loss. Essential for GPUs with <16GB VRAM. Requires 'Compile Model' to be enabled. Note: incompatible with LoRA adapters.
service.mlx_dit_info_enabled: Runs the DiT diffusion model using Apple's native MLX framework instead of PyTorch MPS. Significantly faster on M1/M2/M3/M4 Macs. Recommended for Apple Silicon users.
service.mlx_dit_info_disabled: MLX not available. Requires: macOS + Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4) + mlx package installed. Install with: pip install mlx.
service.tier_info: Override the auto-detected GPU capability tier. Each tier adjusts defaults for offload, quantization, and backend to match your VRAM. Use 'Auto' unless the auto-detection is wrong or you want to force specific optimization settings.

generation.task_type_info: Select the task type for generation.
generation.instruction_info: Instruction is automatically generated based on task type.
generation.track_name_info: Select track name for lego/extract tasks.
generation.track_classes_info: Select multiple track classes for complete task.
generation.lm_codes_info: Paste LM codes hints for text2music generation.
generation.mode_info: Simple = describe in plain language, AI handles the rest (best for quick ideas). Custom = full manual control over caption, lyrics, BPM, key, etc. Remix = upload audio and restyle it with a new caption/lyrics. Repaint = upload audio and regenerate a specific time range. Extract = isolate a single track (vocals, drums, etc). Lego = replace one track in existing audio. Complete = fill in missing tracks.
generation.mode_info_simple: Simple: Describe your music in plain language — AI generates caption, lyrics, BPM, key, and structure for you. Best for quick ideas or if you're not sure what settings to use. Switch to Custom for full manual control.
generation.mode_info_custom: Custom: Full manual control over caption, lyrics, BPM, key, duration, and all generation parameters. Use this when you know exactly what you want. Switch to Simple if you'd rather describe your idea and let AI fill in the details.
generation.mode_info_remix: Remix: Upload source audio and restyle it with your own caption and lyrics. The AI uses the original as a structural guide while applying your new style. Adjust Remix Strength to control how closely it follows the original (high = faithful cover, low = loose reinterpretation).
generation.mode_info_repaint: Repaint: Upload source audio and regenerate only a specific time range (start/end in seconds). Everything outside that range stays untouched. Great for fixing a bad section or replacing a specific part without re-generating the whole track.
generation.mode_info_extract: Extract: Isolate a single track (vocals, drums, bass, etc.) from source audio using AI stem separation. Useful for creating instrumentals, acapellas, or isolating parts for remixing.
generation.mode_info_lego: Lego: Replace a specific track (e.g. drums, vocals) in your source audio while keeping everything else. Upload multi-track audio and choose which stem to regenerate with your new caption/lyrics.
generation.mode_info_complete: Complete: Fill in missing tracks from source audio. If you have a partial mix (e.g. just vocals and guitar), the AI generates the missing parts (drums, bass, etc.) to create a full arrangement.
generation.simple_query_placeholder: Describe the music you want to create, e.g., 'a soft Bengali love song for a quiet evening'. Leave empty for a random sample.
generation.simple_query_info: Enter a natural language description of the music you want to generate.
generation.simple_vocal_language_info: Select preferred language(s) for lyrics. Use 'unknown' for any language.
generation.caption_placeholder: A peaceful acoustic guitar melody with soft vocals.
generation.caption_info: Describe the style, genre, instruments, and mood.
generation.lyrics_placeholder: [Verse 1]\nUnder the starry night\nI feel so alive.
generation.vocal_language_info: Language for generated vocals. Set to match your lyrics language for best results. 'unknown' = let the model decide (good for instrumental or multilingual). 'instrumental' = no vocals.
generation.bpm_info: Tempo of the song. Slow ballad: 60–80. Pop/Rock: 100–130. Dance/EDM: 120–140. Fast punk/metal: 160–200. Leave empty or check 'Auto' to let the model choose based on your caption.
generation.keyscale_placeholder: e.g. C Major, A Minor, F# Minor
generation.keyscale_info: Musical key of the song (e.g. 'C Major', 'Am', 'F# Minor'). Major keys sound bright/happy, minor keys sound dark/sad. Leave empty or check 'Auto' to let the model choose.
generation.timesig_info: Beats per measure. 4 = 4/4 (most pop/rock/electronic). 3 = 3/4 (waltz feel). 6 = 6/8 (compound, flowing feel). 2 = 2/4 (march feel). Use 'N/A' or Auto to let the model decide.
generation.duration_info: Length of the generated audio in seconds. Longer durations need more VRAM and time. -1 = let the model decide based on lyrics/structure. Check 'Auto' for automatic duration.
generation.batch_size_info: How many variations to generate at once. Higher = more options to choose from, but uses more VRAM and takes longer. Generate 2–4 to compare and pick the best result.
generation.inference_steps_info: How many denoising passes the DiT model makes. More steps = higher quality but slower. Turbo models: 4–8 steps (default 8, diminishing returns above 8). SFT models: 30–100 steps (default 50). Base/unknown models default to 32 steps. Doubling steps roughly doubles generation time.
generation.guidance_scale_info: Controls how strictly the output follows your caption/prompt. Higher (10–15) = more faithful to prompt but can sound harsh or distorted. Lower (1–5) = more natural/creative but may drift from your description. Default 7.0. Base model only — has no effect on turbo models.
generation.seed_info: Fixed number for reproducible results — same seed + same settings = same output. Use comma-separated values (e.g. '42,123,456') to set per-sample seeds in a batch. -1 = random.
generation.random_seed_info: When enabled, generates a new random seed each time so every generation is unique. Disable this and set a fixed seed to reproduce a specific result.
generation.audio_format_info: FLAC = lossless, large files. MP3 = small files, slight quality loss. WAV 32-bit = studio quality, largest files. Opus/AAC = efficient lossy compression. Choose FLAC or WAV for production, MP3 for sharing.
generation.use_adg_info: Alternative guidance method that can improve prompt adherence with fewer artifacts than standard CFG. Try enabling if you get distortion at high guidance scales. Experimental — may not always improve results.
generation.shift_info: Controls the noise schedule timing. Higher values (3–5) concentrate denoising effort on early steps (better structure). Lower values (1–2) spread effort more evenly (finer details). Default 3.0. Only affects base models — turbo models ignore this.
generation.infer_method_info: ODE (Euler): Deterministic and faster — same seed always gives same result. Recommended for most use cases. SDE (Stochastic): Adds randomness during diffusion — can produce more varied/creative results but less predictable. Try SDE if ODE results feel too 'safe'.
generation.sampler_mode_info: Euler: Standard first-order solver (fast, default). Heun: Second-order predictor-corrector that evaluates the model twice per step for higher accuracy — produces cleaner audio especially with 8-step turbo inference, at the cost of ~2x diffusion time.
generation.velocity_norm_threshold_info: Clamp velocity prediction norms relative to input norms at each diffusion step. Prevents outlier predictions that cause audio artifacts. 0 = disabled. Values around 1.5–3.0 reduce artifacts. Start with 2.0 if experimenting.
generation.velocity_ema_factor_info: Blend current velocity prediction with the previous step via exponential moving average. Smooths the denoising trajectory for cleaner output. 0 = disabled. Values around 0.05–0.2 work well. Start with 0.1 if experimenting.
generation.custom_timesteps_info: Advanced: Manually define the denoising schedule as comma-separated values from 1.0→0.0 (e.g. '0.97,0.76,0.615,0.5,0.395,0.28,0.18,0.085,0'). Overrides both Inference Steps and Shift. Leave empty to use automatic scheduling. Only change this if you know what you're doing.
generation.cfg_interval_start_info: Controls when CFG guidance kicks in during denoising (0.0 = from the very start, 1.0 = never). Lower values apply guidance for more of the process, giving stronger prompt adherence. Raising this skips guidance in early steps, which can reduce artifacts while still steering the output. Default 0.0. Only affects base models.
generation.cfg_interval_end_info: Controls when CFG guidance stops during denoising (1.0 = at the very end, 0.0 = immediately). Lower values stop guidance early, letting later steps refine freely — can reduce harshness. Higher values maintain guidance longer for stronger prompt control. Default 1.0. Only affects base models.
generation.lm_temperature_info: Controls creativity vs predictability of the 5Hz LM. Lower (0.0–0.5) = safer, more conventional musical choices. Higher (0.8–1.5) = more creative/surprising but riskier. Very high (1.5+) = chaotic, may produce nonsense. Default 0.85 is a good balance. Start here and adjust based on results.
generation.lm_cfg_scale_info: How strongly the LM follows your prompt vs generating freely. 1.0 = no guidance (LM ignores prompt influence). 1.5–2.0 = moderate guidance, good balance. 2.5–3.0 = strong guidance, very prompt-faithful but can reduce variety. 5.0–7.0 = maximum prompt control, may reduce musical quality or introduce artifacts. Values above 7.0 are experimental. Requires a negative prompt to work (set automatically if empty). Only matters when 5Hz LM is active.
generation.lm_top_k_info: Limits LM choices to the K most likely tokens at each step. Lower (10–30) = more focused/predictable output. Higher (50–100) = more diverse. 0 = disabled (no filtering). Works alongside Top-P — both can be active. Start with 0 (disabled) unless you want to constrain diversity.
generation.lm_top_p_info: Keeps only tokens whose cumulative probability reaches P. 0.9 = consider the top 90% most likely tokens (cuts unlikely outliers). Lower (0.5–0.8) = more focused. 1.0 = disabled (consider all tokens). More adaptive than Top-K. Good default: 0.9–0.95.
generation.lm_negative_prompt_placeholder: Enter negative prompt for CFG (default: NO USER INPUT)
generation.lm_negative_prompt_info: Tells the LM what to avoid. Only effective when LM CFG Scale > 1.0. The LM steers away from this prompt and toward your actual caption. Leave empty for the default behavior. Example: 'low quality, noise, distorted' to push away from poor outputs.
generation.cot_metas_info: When enabled, the LM 'thinks through' music metadata (BPM, key, structure) before generating. Produces more coherent results but adds ~2–5s per generation. Disable to skip this step and use your manually set metadata directly. Recommended: keep enabled unless you've set all metadata yourself.
generation.cot_language_info: When enabled, the LM auto-detects the vocal language during chain-of-thought reasoning. Useful when language is ambiguous or set to 'unknown'. Disable if you've explicitly set the vocal language.
generation.constrained_debug_info: Shows detailed logs of how the LM's output tokens are being filtered/constrained. Only useful for developers debugging token generation issues. Leave off for normal use.
generation.auto_score_info: Automatically calculates a quality score (0–1) for each generated audio using perplexity. Higher = better. Useful for comparing results in a batch to find the best one. Adds a few seconds per sample.
generation.auto_lrc_info: Automatically generates timestamped lyrics (LRC format) for each generated audio. Useful for karaoke-style display or syncing lyrics to music. Adds processing time per sample.
generation.lm_batch_chunk_info: How many samples the LM processes at once. Higher = faster batch generation but uses more VRAM. Lower if you get out-of-memory errors during batch generation. Default 8. Reduce to 1–4 on GPUs with <12GB VRAM.
generation.codes_strength_info: What fraction of DiT denoising steps use LM-generated semantic codes as guidance. Higher (0.7–1.0) = output closely follows the LM's musical plan. Lower (0.1–0.4) = DiT has more creative freedom. 0 = codes ignored entirely. Default 1.0 is a good starting point.
generation.cover_strength_info: What fraction of denoising steps reference the source audio structure. Higher = output sounds closer to the original. Lower = more creative reinterpretation. Adjust alongside Cover Strength (Melody Retention) for fine-tuned remix control.
generation.remix_strength_info: How closely the remix follows the source audio's structure and melody. Higher (0.7–1.0) = very close to original, subtle style changes only. Lower (0.1–0.4) = loose interpretation, major creative liberties. 0.5 is a good starting point — adjust up for faithful covers, down for wild remixes.
generation.cover_noise_strength_info: Controls how much of the original melody is preserved during remix. 0 = no melody retention (pure style transfer from your caption). 0.1–0.25 = recommended range with SFT model (restores melody while allowing style changes). 1.0 = maximum melody retention. Higher values keep the tune but may resist style changes — adjust your caption if needed.
generation.enable_normalization_info: Adjusts the final audio volume to a consistent peak level. Prevents clipping (distortion from too-loud audio) and ensures all outputs are at similar loudness. Recommended to keep enabled for consistent results.
generation.normalization_db_info: How loud the normalized audio should be. -1.0 dB = industry standard safe peak (recommended, leaves headroom). -0.1 dB = maximum loudness before clipping. Lower values (e.g. -3.0) = quieter, more headroom for further mixing/mastering.
generation.fade_in_duration_info: Gradually increases volume from silence at the start of the audio. 0 = no fade (starts at full volume). 1–3s = subtle intro. 5–10s = dramatic slow build. Useful for avoiding abrupt starts.
generation.fade_out_duration_info: Gradually decreases volume to silence at the end of the audio. 0 = no fade (may end abruptly). 2–5s = smooth ending. Useful for avoiding harsh cutoffs at the end of generated audio.
generation.latent_shift_info: Advanced: Shifts the internal audio representation before decoding. Try small negative values (e.g. -0.04) if your outputs have clipping/distortion. 0 = no shift (default). Only adjust if you're hearing audio artifacts.
generation.latent_rescale_info: Advanced: Scales the internal audio representation before decoding. Values below 1.0 (e.g. 0.91) can reduce clipping/distortion. 1.0 = no rescale (default). Only adjust if you're hearing audio artifacts despite normalization.
generation.score_sensitivity_info: Controls how the quality score maps to 0–1 range. Lower values (e.g. 0.5) = more spread between scores, easier to distinguish good from bad. Higher values = scores cluster together. Default 1.0. Adjust if all your scores look too similar.
generation.lora_path_info: Path to your trained LoRA adapter directory. Use the Export button in the Training tab to create one, or download a community LoRA.
generation.use_lora_info: Toggle the loaded LoRA adapter on/off during generation. When enabled, the LoRA's trained style is applied. Disable to generate with the base model for comparison.
generation.lora_scale_info: How strongly the LoRA style is applied. 0 = LoRA has no effect (same as base model). 0.5 = blended style. 1.0 = full LoRA effect. Values above 1.0 may over-amplify the style. Start at 1.0 and reduce if the style is too strong.
generation.parallel_thinking_info: When generating multiple samples (batch size > 1), processes all LM thinking in parallel instead of one-by-one. Faster but uses more VRAM. Disable if you get out-of-memory errors with large batches.
generation.caption_rewrite_info: Lets the LM enhance and expand your caption before generation. Your brief description gets rewritten into a more detailed, model-friendly prompt. Useful if you write short captions and want the LM to fill in musical details.
generation.mp3_sample_rate_info: MP3 encoding sample rate. Choose 44.1 kHz or 48 kHz.

results.title: 🎵 Results
results.quality_score_placeholder: Click 'Score' to calculate perplexity-based quality score
results.restore_params_btn: ↙️ Apply These Settings to UI (Restore Batch Parameters)

messages.lm_fallback: Failed to generate example using LM, falling back to examples directory
messages.lm_not_initialized: ❌ 5Hz LM not initialized. Please initialize it first.
messages.think_requires_lm: ⚠️ 'Think' requires 5Hz LM to be initialized. Think has been disabled — generation will proceed without LM thinking.
messages.autogen_enabled: 🔄 AutoGen enabled - next batch will generate after this
messages.no_batch_data: No batch data found to restore.
messages.no_codes: ❌ No audio codes available. Please generate music first.
messages.lrc_save_memory_disabled: ❌ LRC generation is disabled in save-memory mode (ACESTEP_SAVE_MEMORY=1). Intermediate tensors are not stored to reduce RAM usage.
messages.lrc_no_batch_data: ❌ No batch data found. Please generate music first.
messages.lrc_no_extra_outputs: ❌ No extra outputs found. Condition tensors not available.
messages.lrc_missing_tensors: ❌ Missing required tensors for LRC generation.
messages.lrc_sample_not_exist: ❌ Sample does not exist in current batch.
messages.sample_creation_failed: ❌ Failed to create sample. Please try again.
messages.sample_created: ✅ Sample created! Review the caption and lyrics, then click Generate Music.
messages.simple_examples_not_found: ⚠️ Simple mode examples directory not found.
messages.simple_examples_empty: ⚠️ No example files found in simple mode examples.
messages.skipping_metas_cot: ⚡ Skipping Phase 1 metas COT (sample already formatted)
messages.invalid_timesteps_format: ⚠️ Invalid timesteps format. Using default schedule.
messages.timesteps_out_of_range: ⚠️ Timesteps must be in range [0, 1]. Using default schedule.
messages.timesteps_count_mismatch: ⚠️ Timesteps count ({actual}) differs from inference_steps ({expected}). Using timesteps count.
messages.audio_format_invalid: {role} format is invalid or unsupported. Please upload a valid audio file.

training.scan_info: Scan for audio files (wav, mp3, flac, ogg, opus).
training.step2_instruction:
Click the button below to use AI to automatically generate metadata for all audio files:
- **Caption**: Music style, genre, mood description
- **BPM**: Beats per minute
- **Key**: Music key (e.g. C Major, Am)
- **Time Signature**: 4/4, 3/4 etc.
training.step5_intro:
**Preprocessing converts your dataset into pre-computed tensors for fast training.**

You can:
- Use the dataset from steps 1-4 above, **OR**
- Load an existing dataset JSON file (if you have one saved)
training.step5_details:
This step will:
- Encode audio to VAE latents
- Encode captions and lyrics to text embeddings
- Run condition encoders
- Save all tensors to `.pt` files

⚠️ **This requires loading models and may take a few minutes.**
training.train_tensor_selection_desc:
Select the directory containing preprocessed tensor files (`.pt` files).
These are created using the 'Preprocess' button in the 'Dataset Builder' tab.
training.all_instrumental_info: Check if all tracks are instrumental (no vocals).
training.custom_tag_info: Unique tag to activate this LoRA style.
training.tag_position_info: Where to place the custom tag in the caption.
training.genre_ratio_info: 0%=All Caption, 100%=All Genre. Single sample override takes precedence.
training.skip_metas_info: Skip BPM/Key/TimeSig generation. Captions and genres are still generated by LM.
training.only_unlabeled_info: Only label samples with no caption (for continuing failed labeling).
training.select_sample_info: Select sample to preview and edit.
training.caption: Caption
training.prompt_override_info: Override global ratio for this sample.
training.load_existing_info: Path to previously saved dataset JSON file.
training.tensor_output_info: Directory to save preprocessed tensor files.
training.output_dir_info: Directory to save trained LoRA weights.
training.lokr_no_checkpoints_use_latest: ℹ No checkpoints found; export will use latest available weights
training.lokr_no_exportable_checkpoints: ℹ No exportable epoch checkpoints found
training.lokr_no_weights_selected_epoch: ❌ No LoKr weights found for selected epoch: {epoch}
training.lokr_no_weights_latest_checkpoint: ❌ No LoKr weights found in latest checkpoint: {checkpoint}
training.lokr_no_trained_weights_found: ❌ No trained LoKr weights found in {path}
training.lokr_tensor_selection_desc:
Select the directory containing preprocessed tensor files (`.pt` files).
These are created using the 'Preprocess' button in the 'Dataset Builder' tab.
training.lokr_linear_alpha_info: Scaling factor for LoKr (usually similar to dim).
training.lokr_decompose_both_info: When enabled, decomposes both left and right matrices.
training.lokr_use_scalar_info: Enable scalar gating for LoKr weights.
training.lokr_weight_decompose_info: Enable weight decomposition for more stable LoKr training.
training.lokr_learning_rate_info: LoKr commonly uses a higher LR than LoRA. Tune per dataset.
training.lokr_output_dir_info: Directory to save trained LoKr weights.
training.lokr_checkpoint_epoch_info: Select a specific epoch checkpoint to export, or keep Latest (auto).

help.getting_started:
## Getting Started

1. **Select a model** in the Settings accordion (e.g. `acestep-v15-turbo`)
2. **Choose a 5Hz LM** if you want Thinking mode (recommended)
3. Click **Initialize Service** and wait for the green status
4. Pick a **Generation Mode** (start with Simple)
5. Describe your music and click **Generate Music**

> **Tip:** Turbo models are fastest. Enable "Think" for smarter generation.
help.service_config:
## Service Configuration

### Quick Setup
1. Select **Main Model Path** (turbo recommended)
2. Select **5Hz LM Model Path** (auto-filtered by GPU)
3. Choose **Backend**: `vllm` (fast, NVIDIA ≥8GB) or `pt` (universal)
4. Check **Initialize 5Hz LM** for Thinking mode
5. Click **Initialize Service**

### Performance Tips
- **Flash Attention**: Faster inference (needs flash_attn)
- **CPU Offload**: Auto-enabled on GPUs <20GB
- **INT8 Quantization**: Reduces VRAM, auto-enabled <20GB
- **Compile**: Required for quantization, enabled by default

### LoRA
- Set LoRA path → Load → Enable "Use LoRA"
- ⚠️ Cannot use LoRA with INT8 quantization
help.generation_simple:
## Simple Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Quick music creation with minimal effort.

### Steps
1. Select **Simple** in Generation Mode
2. Type a description, e.g. *"upbeat pop song with catchy guitar riff"*
3. (Optional) Check **Instrumental** for no vocals
4. (Optional) Select **Vocal Language**
5. Click **Create Sample** — AI generates caption, lyrics, metadata
6. Review and edit the generated content if needed
7. Click **Generate Music**

### Tips
- Click 🎲 for random inspiration
- The more specific your description, the better the result
- Leave BPM/Key empty to let AI decide
help.generation_custom:
## Custom Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Full creative control over every parameter.

### Steps
1. Select **Custom** in Generation Mode
2. Write a detailed **Caption** describing style, genre, instruments, mood
3. Write **Lyrics** with structure tags: `[Verse]`, `[Chorus]`, `[Bridge]`
4. (Optional) Upload **Reference Audio** for style guidance
5. Set **BPM**, **Key**, **Duration** or leave empty for auto
6. Click **Format** to enhance with LM (optional)
7. Click **Generate Music**

### Caption Tips
- Be specific: *"dreamy shoegaze with reverb-heavy guitars and whispered vocals"*
- Include: genre, instruments, mood, tempo feel, vocal style
help.generation_remix:
## Remix Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Creating cover versions or style transfers.

### Steps
1. Select **Remix** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio** (the song to remix)
3. Write a **Caption** describing the target style
4. (Optional) Modify **Lyrics**
5. Adjust **Remix Strength** (0.0–1.0):
   - Higher = closer to original structure
   - Lower = more creative freedom
6. Click **Generate Music**

### Tips
- Start with strength 0.5 and adjust
- Use SFT model with Cover Strength 0.1–0.25 for melody retention
help.generation_repaint:
## Repaint Mode Tutorial

**Best for:** Fixing or regenerating specific sections of generated music while keeping the rest intact.

### Steps
1. Select **Repaint** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio**
3. Set **Repainting Start** (seconds) and **End** (-1 for end of file)
4. Write a **Caption** for the repainted section
5. Choose a **Repaint Mode** and adjust **Repaint Strength** if needed
6. Click **Generate Music**

### Repaint Mode
Controls how the model treats non-repainted regions:

- **Conservative** (Strength = 0): Maximum source preservation. Source latents are injected at every diffusion step with long boundary crossfades. Best for seamless blending.
- **Balanced** (default): Source latents are injected for a fraction of steps controlled by the Repaint Strength slider. Good starting point.
- **Aggressive** (Strength = 1): No source injection at all — pure diffusion. Maximum creative freedom but less boundary consistency.

### Repaint Strength
Only active in **Balanced** mode. Drag to control the preserve-vs-regenerate tradeoff:
- **0** = Conservative behavior (auto-switches mode)
- **0.5** = Default — inject source during first 50% of steps
- **1** = Aggressive behavior (auto-switches mode)

### Tips
- Use "Send To Repaint" from Results to quickly load audio
- Start with Balanced (0.5) and adjust based on results
- Lower strength for better boundary consistency, higher for more creative output
help.generation_extract:
## Extract Mode (Base Model Only)

**Best for:** Stem separation — isolating instruments from a mix.

### Steps
1. Select **Extract** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio**
3. Select **Track Name** to extract (e.g. vocals, drums, bass)
4. Click **Extract Stem**

### Available Tracks
vocals, backing_vocals, drums, bass, guitar, keyboard, percussion, strings, synth, fx, brass, woodwinds
help.generation_lego:
## Lego Mode (Base Model Only)

**Best for:** Adding new instrument tracks to existing audio.

### Steps
1. Select **Lego** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio**
3. Select **Track Name** to add
4. Write a **Caption** describing the track
5. Click **Add Stem**

### Tips
- Great for layering: add drums to a guitar track, add bass to vocals
- The caption should describe only the new track's characteristics
help.generation_complete:
## Complete Mode (Base Model Only)

**Best for:** Auto-arranging — filling in missing instruments.

### Steps
1. Select **Complete** in Generation Mode
2. Upload **Source Audio** (partial arrangement)
3. Select multiple **Track Names** to add
4. Write a **Caption** describing the desired style
5. Click **Generate Music**
help.generation_caption:
## Writing Good Captions

### Structure
A good caption includes:
- **Genre/Style**: pop, rock, jazz, electronic, classical…
- **Instruments**: guitar, piano, synth, drums, strings…
- **Mood**: upbeat, melancholic, energetic, dreamy…
- **Vocal style**: whispered, powerful, falsetto, rap…
- **Tempo feel**: fast, slow, moderate, driving…

### Examples
- *"Energetic pop-punk with distorted guitars, fast drums, and shouted vocals"*
- *"Smooth jazz trio with walking bass, brushed drums, and mellow piano"*
- *"Ambient electronic with layered synth pads and no vocals"*

### Tips
- More detail = better results
- Use the **Format** button to let AI enhance your caption
- Check 🎲 for example captions
help.generation_lyrics:
## Writing Lyrics

### Structure Tags
Use section tags to structure your song:
```
[Verse 1]
Your verse lyrics here

[Chorus]
Your chorus lyrics here

[Verse 2]
Second verse here

[Bridge]
Bridge section

[Outro]
Ending lyrics
```

### Tips
- Keep verses 4–8 lines
- Choruses should be memorable and repetitive
- Use `[Instrumental]` or `[Interlude]` for non-vocal sections
- Check **Instrumental** checkbox for pure instrumental music
- Select **Vocal Language** to match your lyrics language
help.generation_advanced:
## Advanced Settings

### Key Parameters
- **Inference Steps**: Turbo=8 (default), Base=up to 200. More steps ≠ always better for turbo
- **Guidance Scale**: Base model only. Higher = follows prompt more strictly
- **Shift**: Timestep shift (1.0–5.0). 3.0 recommended for turbo
- **Seed**: Set a specific seed for reproducible results

### LM Parameters
- **Temperature** (0.0–2.0): Higher = more creative/random
- **CFG Scale** (1.0–3.0): Higher = follows prompt more
- **Top-K / Top-P**: Sampling strategies for diversity

### Think Mode
Enable **Think** to use 5Hz LM for smarter generation:
- Generates semantic codes and metadata
- Requires LM to be initialized
- **ParallelThinking**: Process batches in parallel (faster)
help.results:
## Results Section

### Per-Sample Controls
- **Audio Player**: Play, pause, download
- **Send To Remix/Repaint**: Use this result as source for further editing
- **Save**: Export audio + metadata as JSON
- **Score**: Calculate quality score (perplexity-based)
- **LRC**: Generate lyrics timestamps

### Batch Navigation
- Use **◀ Previous** / **Next ▶** to browse batches
- Enable **AutoGen** to auto-generate next batch
- Click **Apply These Settings to UI** to reuse parameters from a good result

### Tips
- Generate 2–4 variations (batch size) and pick the best
- Use Score to objectively compare results
- Save good results for reference
help.training_dataset:
## Dataset Builder Tutorial

### Step 1: Load or Scan
- **Load**: Enter path to existing dataset JSON → Click Load
- **Scan**: Enter audio folder path → Click Scan
  - Supported: wav, mp3, flac, ogg, opus

### Step 2: Configure
- Set **Dataset Name**
- Check **All Instrumental** if no vocals
- Set **Custom Activation Tag** (unique trigger word for your LoRA)
- Choose **Tag Position**: Prepend, Append, or Replace

### Step 3: Auto-Label
- Click **Auto-Label All** to generate captions, BPM, key, time sig
- Use **Skip Metas** to skip BPM/Key/TimeSig (faster)

### Step 4: Preview & Edit
- Use slider to browse samples
- Edit caption, lyrics, BPM, key manually
- Click **Save Changes** per sample

### Step 5: Save
- Enter save path → Click **Save Dataset**

### Step 6: Preprocess
- Set tensor output directory → Click **Preprocess**
- This encodes audio/text to tensors for training

### 📖 Documentation
- [LoRA Training Tutorial](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/en/LoRA_Training_Tutorial.md) — Full step-by-step guide
- [Side-Step Advanced Training](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/sidestep/Getting%20Started.md) — CLI-based training with advanced features
help.training_train:
## LoRA Training Tutorial

### Setup
1. Enter **Preprocessed Tensors Directory** → Click **Load Dataset**
2. Configure LoRA:
   - **Rank** (r): 64 default. Higher = more capacity
   - **Alpha**: Usually 2× rank (128)
   - **Dropout**: 0.1 for regularization

### Training
3. Set **Learning Rate** (start with 1e-4)
4. Set **Max Epochs** (500 default)
5. Click **Start Training**
6. Monitor loss curve — it should decrease over time
7. Click **Stop Training** when satisfied

### Export
8. Enter export path → Click **Export LoRA**
9. Load in Settings: set LoRA Path → Load LoRA → Enable Use LoRA

### 🚀 Try LoKr for Faster Training
LoKr has greatly improved training efficiency. What used to take an hour now only takes 5 minutes — **over 10× faster**. This is crucial for training on consumer-grade GPUs. Switch to the **Train LoKr** tab to get started.

### Tips
- Use small batch size (1) if VRAM is limited
- Gradient accumulation increases effective batch size
- Save checkpoints frequently (every 200 epochs)

### 📖 Documentation
- [LoRA Training Tutorial](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/en/LoRA_Training_Tutorial.md) — Full step-by-step guide
- [Side-Step Advanced Training](https://github.com/ACE-Step/ACE-Step-1.5/blob/main/docs/sidestep/Getting%20Started.md) — CLI training with corrected timesteps, LoKR, VRAM optimization
help.training_lokr:
## 🚀 LoKr Training Tutorial

LoKr (Low-rank Kronecker product) has greatly improved training efficiency. What used to take an hour with LoRA now only takes 5 minutes — **over 10× faster**. This is crucial for training on consumer-grade GPUs.

### Setup
1. Enter **Preprocessed Tensors Directory** → Click **Load Dataset**
2. Configure LoKr:
   - **Linear Dim**: 64 default (similar to LoRA rank)
   - **Linear Alpha**: 128 default (scaling factor)
   - **Weight Decompose (DoRA)**: Enabled by default for better quality

### Training
3. Set **Learning Rate** (LoKr commonly uses higher LR, start with 1e-3)
4. Set **Max Epochs** (500 default)
5. Click **Start LoKr Training**
6. Monitor loss curve — it should decrease over time
7. Click **Stop Training** when satisfied

### Export
8. Enter export path → Click **Export LoKr**
9. Load in Settings: set LoRA Path → Load LoRA → Enable Use LoRA

### LoKr vs LoRA
| | LoKr | LoRA |
|---|---|---|
| Speed | ⚡ ~10× faster | Slower |
| VRAM | Lower | Higher |
| Quality | Comparable | Baseline |
| Best for | Consumer GPUs, rapid iteration | Maximum fidelity |

### Tips
- LoKr uses Kronecker decomposition for extreme efficiency
- Enable **DoRA** (Weight Decompose) for improved quality
- Use **Tucker decomposition** for additional compression
- Higher learning rate (1e-3) often works better than LoRA's typical 1e-4
help.training_export:
## Using Your Trained LoRA

### Export
1. After training, enter export path
2. Click **Export LoRA**

### Load & Use
1. In Settings, set **LoRA Path** to your exported directory
2. Click **Load LoRA**
3. Enable **Use LoRA** checkbox
4. Generate music — your LoRA style will be applied

### Tips
- Use your **Custom Activation Tag** in captions to trigger the style
- ⚠️ LoRA is incompatible with INT8 quantization
- You can unload and switch between different LoRAs

gen.enable_normalization_info: Adjusts the final audio volume to a consistent peak level. Prevents clipping (distortion from too-loud audio) and ensures all outputs are at similar loudness. Recommended to keep enabled for consistent results.
gen.normalization_db_info: How loud the normalized audio should be. -1.0 dB = industry standard safe peak (recommended, leaves headroom). -0.1 dB = maximum loudness before clipping. Lower values (e.g. -3.0) = quieter, more headroom for further mixing/mastering.
gen.fade_in_duration_info: Gradually increases volume from silence at the start of the audio. 0 = no fade (starts at full volume). 1–3s = subtle intro. 5–10s = dramatic slow build. Useful for avoiding abrupt starts.
gen.fade_out_duration_info: Gradually decreases volume to silence at the end of the audio. 0 = no fade (may end abruptly). 2–5s = smooth ending. Useful for avoiding harsh cutoffs at the end of generated audio.
gen.latent_shift_info: Advanced: Shifts the internal audio representation before decoding. Try small negative values (e.g. -0.04) if your outputs have clipping/distortion. 0 = no shift (default). Only adjust if you're hearing audio artifacts.
gen.latent_rescale_info: Advanced: Scales the internal audio representation before decoding. Values below 1.0 (e.g. 0.91) can reduce clipping/distortion. 1.0 = no rescale (default). Only adjust if you're hearing audio artifacts despite normalization.
